content_en:
“ Aba Dawid #4985,

Chapter Tivo: The Secret of Salah les in devotion to Allah

are filled with everything except Allah. T’hus, praying merely keeps them from indulging in this worldly life that they love so much. They are easily observed from their manner of performing prayer; they pray in a rush and lack tranquility and calmness. The only thing that compels them to pray in the first place is that they know it is a must (obligation). However, their salah is deficient; they utter with their tongues that which does not conform to what is in their hearts. All the while, the voice of their heart echos, let’s get this sa/db over with! Therefore the difference between a petson whose comfort is found in the salah and a person whose comfort is found outside the salah is obvious and self-evident. The former finds salah to be a grace upon his heart through which he experiences tranquility, calmness and pleasure, whereas the latter finds salah akin to heavy chains shackling his limbs and a gloomy cell imprisoning his heart.

"Truly, those whose comfort is not found in saléh feel as if praying was a prison for their soul and chains confining their limbs from committing sins. Despite that, the salah of this type of people might be a means whereby their sins are forgiven and they receive a share of Allah’s Mercy depending on how much servitude to Allah they manage to demonstrate while praying. But on the other hand, it is also possible they might be punished for the deficiency in their salah. As for those whose comfort is to be found in salah, for them praying is the garden in which they find comfort, pleasure, tranquility, and the grace of their hearts and souls. It brings them nearer to Allah and elevates their rank, so that they only have a reward similar to those that preceded, but they also enjoy a special rank that none ever had, by being brought near to Allah, in addition to the mete reward they receive for the performance of their salah.

INNER DIMENSIONS OF THE PRAYER

2.7 From the benefits of Sa/ah is nearness to Allah

It is customary for kings to promise reward and nearness to those whom they are pleased with. The magicians said to pharaoh,

Heisei abs “Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?”

and he answered that they would be rewarded and be among those near to him, saying OWNS KGS 556 “Yes, and, [moreover], you will be among those made

The example of the first type is like a man who entered the abode of the king but was unable to gain entrance to him as there was a barrier standing between him and the king, hence he was unable to see him or gain access to this person. The battier is an embodiment of his desires and lusts as well as the smoke screen of his hopeful wishes in this life; his heart is sick and his self is wrapped up in what it desires wanting only its immediate share in this life. It is for these reasons that all the while they pray they are in an unhappy state wherein they are not only unable to obtain any comfort, but are also empty of feelings of fear and hope in Allah, which makes prayer a suffering for them that only ceases when they finish praying, as only then are they able to return to that in which they find their comfort (i.e. worldly affairs and pleasures),

Chapter Two: The Secret of Salah lies in devotion to Allah

The example of the second type is like a man who entered the house of the king upon which the barrier screening him from the king is removed; thus he is able to find delight in looking at the king and being at his service and in his obedience. In return, the king lavishes upon him every form of grace and brings him near to himself, For all these reasons, he is unable to bear leaving the house and wishes to remain standing before him to enjoy the sweetness of being close to him, the tranquility that he experiences from it, and being held in esteem by Allah while he basks in His good Words (i.e. Qur’an). He also enjoys being in such a state of humbleness and humility before Him, for which he invokes Him more and more while he is being showered with His graces from every direction, not to mention the calmness that his soul experiences while his heart and limbs are fully attentive to his Lord. He is pleased and comfortable, worshipping Allah as if he sees Him, for He manifests Himself to him through His Words (.e. Que’An). It should thus come as no surprise that the most distressing thing for him is to have to leave all that (i.e. conclude the salah). And Allah is the Guide and the Helper.

‘The aforementioned are just some brief glimpses and hints about

saléh and a disclosure of some of its subtle qualities and hidden gems.

Distinction between the people of a/-Sama‘and the people of Salah

I call upon the people (a/Sama') who indulge themselves in listening (to music and songs) and I ask them by Allah, the One and only truthful ah worthy of worship, to tell me if the experience and enjoyment they attain from listening to songs could ever be equal to, or even vaguely similar to, such feelings and state of heart! Moreover, I ask them, by Allah, to tell me whether their indulgence in songs would allow them at all to experience these sublime emotions and state of heart when they pray? In fact, I would like to know if they have ever even detected from afar the fragrance of such sublime emotions and experiences!

I will take the liberty of answering these questions on their behalf, and I swear by Allah that the experience and enjoyment attained from praying is incongruous with, and bears not an iota of resemblance to the experience and enjoyment they attain from listening to songs.(I fear that I might end up writing a long chapter, otherwise I would have mentioned some of what they experience when listening to songs, in order to explain the nature of this type of listening.)

A person whose heart has life in it and who enjoys even a mini-

Chapter Three: Distinction between the people of alSama‘ and the people

mal level of understanding shall realise the difference between the experience and enjoyment attained from reciting the Words of Allah (.c. Qur’in) and the experience and enjoyment attained from singing lyrics. And he shall recognise the difference between the experience and enjoyment attained from standing before Allah, the Lord of all the worlds (in sa/ah) and the experience and enjoyment attained from standing before singers (and musicians). He will also understand the difference between the experience and enjoyment attained from comprehending the meanings of the Words of Allah and the experience and enjoyment they attain from singing and enrapturement, which is the spell of fornication and the Qur’an of Satan. By Allah, whenever the love of both tastes (ic. Qur’an and songs) enters the heart of a petson, only one of them can remain in the heart as one of them will expel the other; just as the daughter of the Messenger of Allah (4g) and the daughter of the enemy of Allah cannot ever be married at the same time to one man." And Allah, Exalted be He, Knows best.

“| He quotes a part of a hadith in which the Prophet (3) disliked ‘All ibn Abi ‘Talib to marry the daughter of Abii Jabl.

Bukhari #2943 and Muslim #2449. This part is used as a metaphor to mean that the words of Allah and the words of Satan cannot gather in one place.

It is only when the hearts that have deviated far from the guidance of the Prophet (ig) and have abandoned what he, his Companions and the righteous Salaf were upon, become upright that they will regain the discernment through which they can experience the right enjoyment and sensation. ‘Ihe early generations used to find enjoyment in matters pertaining to Allah, Exalted be He, such as: praying, reciting the Qur’an, contemplating on the Words of Allah and listening to them, attending the assemblies of scholars and the gatherings of knowledge, fighting in battles for the sake of Allah, enjoining the good and forbidding the wrongdoing, loving and despising people for the sake of Allah, etc. However, the preference of the later generations, except those whom Allah saved, became distorted and they sought enjoyment in musical instruments, songs, dancing, noise and clamour, while desisting from doing deeds that Allah loves. Indeed, the experience and enjoyment attained from melodies is nothing like the experience and enjoyment attained from the Qur’an. The experience and enjoyment attained from musical instruments is nothing like the experience and enjoyment attained from reciting a/-Nir? and

al-My’minin. The experience and enjoyment attained from tunes is nothing like the experience and enjoyment attained from the a/- Zumar, and the expetience and enjoyment obtained from flutes is nothing like the experience and enjoyment attained from the aah,

“The Hour has come neat, and the moon has split [in two].”

“ Surah al-Saffit (37)

INNER DIMENSIONS OF THE PRAYER

The expetience and enjoyment of those who stand on their feet serving Satan is in nothing at all like the experience and enjoyment of those who stand on their feet serving the most Merciful while reciting a/-An‘an and al-ArGpf'.The experience and enjoyment of those in rapture upon being overwhelmed by music is nothing like the experience and enjoyment of the knower (“rif) when listening to the Great Qur’an and, in particular, when listening to a/-Fatibab. And, the experience and enjoyment of those who jostle in Satan’s barn to listen to music is nothing like the experience and enjoyment of those who crowd before the Most Merciful.
content_id:
Abu Dawud #4985,

Bab Dua: Rahasia Salah Terletak pada Penghambaan kepada Allah

dipenuhi dengan segala sesuatu selain Allah. Dengan demikian, salah hanya sekadar menahan mereka dari menikmati kehidupan dunia yang sangat mereka cintai. Hal ini mudah diamati dari cara mereka menunaikan salah; mereka salah dengan tergesa-gesa dan tidak memiliki ketenangan serta kekhusyukan. Satu-satunya hal yang mendorong mereka salah sejak awal adalah karena mereka tahu bahwa itu adalah kewajiban. Namun, salah mereka penuh kekurangan; mereka mengucapkan dengan lisan mereka apa yang tidak sesuai dengan apa yang ada di hati mereka. Sementara itu, suara hati mereka bergema, ayo cepat selesaikan salah ini! Oleh karena itu, perbedaan antara orang yang menemukan kenyamanannya dalam salah dan orang yang menemukan kenyamanannya di luar salah sangatlah jelas dan nyata. Yang pertama mendapati salah sebagai anugerah bagi hatinya, melaluinya ia merasakan ketenangan, kedamaian, dan kesenangan, sedangkan yang kedua mendapati salah bagaikan rantai berat yang membelenggu anggota tubuhnya dan sel gelap yang memenjarakan hatinya.

Sesungguhnya, mereka yang kenyamanannya tidak ditemukan dalam salah merasa seolah-olah salah adalah penjara bagi jiwa mereka dan belenggu yang mengekang anggota tubuh mereka dari berbuat dosa. Meskipun demikian, salah orang-orang jenis ini mungkin menjadi sarana pengampunan dosa-dosa mereka dan mereka mendapat bagian dari rahmat Allah, tergantung seberapa besar penghambaan kepada Allah yang berhasil mereka tunjukkan saat salah. Namun di sisi lain, bisa juga mereka mendapat hukuman karena kekurangan dalam salah mereka. Adapun mereka yang kenyamanannya ditemukan dalam salah, bagi mereka salah adalah taman tempat mereka menemukan kenyamanan, kesenangan, ketenangan, serta anugerah bagi hati dan jiwa mereka. Salah mendekatkan mereka kepada Allah dan mengangkat derajat mereka, sehingga mereka tidak hanya mendapat pahala seperti orang-orang sebelumnya, tetapi mereka juga menikmati kedudukan istimewa yang tidak pernah dimiliki siapa pun, yaitu didekatkan kepada Allah, di samping pahala yang mereka terima atas pelaksanaan salah mereka.

DIMENSI BATIN SALAH

2.7 Di antara manfaat salah adalah kedekatan kepada Allah

Sudah menjadi kebiasaan para raja untuk menjanjikan pahala dan kedekatan kepada mereka yang diridhai. Para tukang sihir berkata kepada Fir'aun,

"Apakah sesungguhnya kami akan mendapat imbalan jika kami yang menang?"

dan ia menjawab bahwa mereka akan diberi imbalan dan termasuk di antara orang-orang yang didekatkan kepadanya, dengan berkata, "Ya, dan kalian pasti termasuk orang-orang yang didekatkan."

Perumpamaan jenis pertama bagaikan seorang pria yang memasuki kediaman raja tetapi tidak mampu menemui raja karena ada penghalang yang berdiri di antara dirinya dan sang raja, sehingga ia tidak dapat melihatnya atau mendapatkan akses kepada beliau. Penghalang itu adalah perwujudan dari hawa nafsu dan syahwatnya serta tabir harapan-harapannya dalam kehidupan ini; hatinya sakit dan jiwanya terbalut dalam apa yang diinginkannya, hanya menginginkan bagian sesaatnya di dunia ini. Karena alasan-alasan inilah sepanjang mereka salah, mereka berada dalam keadaan tidak bahagia di mana mereka tidak hanya tidak mampu memperoleh kenyamanan apa pun, tetapi juga kosong dari perasaan takut dan harap kepada Allah, yang menjadikan salah sebagai penderitaan bagi mereka yang baru berhenti ketika mereka selesai salah, karena barulah saat itu mereka bisa kembali kepada hal yang membuat mereka nyaman (yaitu urusan dan kesenangan duniawi).

Bab Dua: Rahasia Salah Terletak pada Penghambaan kepada Allah

Perumpamaan jenis kedua bagaikan seorang pria yang memasuki rumah raja, lalu tabir yang menghalanginya dari sang raja pun disingkapkan; sehingga ia dapat menemukan kesenangan dalam memandang sang raja, melayaninya, dan berada dalam ketaatannya. Sebagai balasannya, sang raja melimpahkan segala bentuk anugerah kepadanya dan mendekatkannya kepada dirinya. Karena semua alasan ini, ia tidak sanggup meninggalkan rumah itu dan berharap untuk tetap berdiri di hadapannya guna menikmati manisnya kedekatan dengannya, ketenangan yang ia rasakan darinya, dan dimuliakan oleh Allah sementara ia bermandikan firman-firman-Nya yang indah (yaitu al-Quran). Ia juga menikmati berada dalam keadaan tunduk dan rendah hati di hadapan-Nya, untuk itulah ia semakin banyak berdoa kepada-Nya sementara ia dilimpahi anugerah-Nya dari segala arah, belum lagi ketenangan yang dirasakan jiwanya sementara hati dan anggota tubuhnya sepenuhnya tertuju kepada Tuhannya. Ia merasa senang dan nyaman, beribadah kepada Allah seolah-olah ia melihat-Nya, karena Dia menampakkan diri-Nya kepadanya melalui firman-firman-Nya (yaitu al-Quran). Maka tidak mengherankan jika hal yang paling menyedihkan baginya adalah harus meninggalkan semua itu (yaitu mengakhiri salah). Dan Allah-lah Pemberi Petunjuk dan Penolong.

Yang telah disebutkan di atas hanyalah sekilas gambaran dan isyarat tentang

salah serta pengungkapan sebagian dari kualitas-kualitasnya yang halus dan permata-permatanya yang tersembunyi.

Perbedaan antara ahli al-Sama' dan ahli Salah

Aku menyeru kepada orang-orang (al-Sama') yang memanjakan diri mereka dalam mendengarkan (musik dan nyanyian) dan aku meminta mereka demi Allah, Yang Maha Esa dan satu-satunya yang benar-benar layak disembah, untuk memberitahuku apakah pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang mereka peroleh dari mendengarkan nyanyian bisa setara dengan, atau bahkan sedikit mirip dengan, perasaan dan keadaan hati seperti itu! Lebih jauh lagi, aku meminta mereka, demi Allah, untuk memberitahuku apakah keasyikan mereka dalam nyanyian akan memungkinkan mereka sama sekali untuk merasakan emosi dan keadaan hati yang luhur ini ketika mereka salah? Bahkan, aku ingin tahu apakah mereka pernah menangkap dari kejauhan aroma emosi dan pengalaman luhur seperti itu!

Aku akan mengambil kebebasan untuk menjawab pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini atas nama mereka, dan aku bersumpah demi Allah bahwa pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari salah tidak sebanding dengan, dan tidak memiliki secuil pun kemiripan dengan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang mereka peroleh dari mendengarkan nyanyian. (Aku khawatir tulisan ini menjadi bab yang panjang, jika tidak aku akan menyebutkan sebagian dari apa yang mereka alami ketika mendengarkan nyanyian, untuk menjelaskan sifat dari jenis mendengarkan ini.)

Seseorang yang hatinya memiliki kehidupan di dalamnya dan yang menikmati sedikit

Bab Tiga: Perbedaan antara ahli al-Sama' dan ahli

saja tingkat pemahaman akan menyadari perbedaan antara pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari membaca firman Allah (yaitu al-Quran) dan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari lirik nyanyian. Dan ia akan mengenali perbedaan antara pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari berdiri di hadapan Allah, Tuhan semesta alam (dalam salah) dan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari berdiri di hadapan para penyanyi (dan pemusik). Ia juga akan memahami perbedaan antara pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari memahami makna-makna firman Allah dan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang mereka peroleh dari nyanyian dan keasyikan, yang merupakan mantra perzinaan dan al-Quran-nya setan. Demi Allah, setiap kali kecintaan terhadap kedua rasa itu (yaitu al-Quran dan nyanyian) memasuki hati seseorang, hanya salah satu yang dapat bertahan di dalam hati karena yang satu akan mengusir yang lain; sebagaimana putri Rasulullah (ﷺ) dan putri musuh Allah tidak pernah bisa dinikahi oleh satu orang pria pada waktu yang bersamaan." Dan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi Maha Mengetahui.

Beliau mengutip bagian dari hadith di mana Nabi (ﷺ) tidak menyukai Ali ibn Abi Talib menikahi putri Abu Jahl.

Bukhari #2943 dan Muslim #2449. Bagian ini digunakan sebagai metafora yang berarti bahwa firman Allah dan perkataan setan tidak dapat berkumpul di satu tempat.

Hanyalah ketika hati-hati yang telah menyimpang jauh dari petunjuk Nabi (ﷺ) dan telah meninggalkan apa yang beliau, para Sahabatnya, dan para Salaf yang saleh jalani, menjadi lurus kembali, barulah mereka akan mendapatkan kembali ketajaman mata hati yang dengannya mereka dapat merasakan kenikmatan dan perasaan yang benar. Generasi awal dahulu menemukan kenikmatan dalam hal-hal yang berkaitan dengan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi, seperti: salah, membaca al-Quran, merenungkan firman Allah dan mendengarkannya, menghadiri majelis para ulama dan halaqah ilmu, berjihad di jalan Allah, memerintahkan yang ma'ruf dan mencegah yang mungkar, mencintai dan membenci orang demi Allah, dan sebagainya. Akan tetapi, kecenderungan generasi belakangan, kecuali mereka yang Allah selamatkan, menjadi menyimpang dan mereka mencari kenikmatan dalam alat-alat musik, nyanyian, tarian, kebisingan dan kegaduhan, sementara menjauhkan diri dari melakukan amalan-amalan yang Allah cintai. Sungguh, pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari melodi tidak ada apa-apanya dibandingkan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari al-Quran. Pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari alat-alat musik tidak ada apa-apanya dibandingkan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari membaca surah al-Nur dan

surah al-Mu'minun. Pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari irama tidak ada apa-apanya dibandingkan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari surah al-Zumar, dan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari seruling tidak ada apa-apanya dibandingkan pengalaman dan kenikmatan yang diperoleh dari salah,

"Saat (kiamat) telah dekat dan bulan pun telah terbelah."

Surah al-Saffat (37)

DIMENSI BATIN SALAH

Pengalaman dan kenikmatan mereka yang berdiri di atas kaki mereka melayani setan sama sekali tidak ada apa-apanya dibandingkan pengalaman dan kenikmatan mereka yang berdiri di atas kaki mereka melayani Yang Maha Pengasih sambil membaca surah al-An'am dan surah al-A'raf. Pengalaman dan kenikmatan mereka yang terlena ketika diliputi musik sama sekali tidak ada apa-apanya dibandingkan pengalaman dan kenikmatan seorang yang mengenal Allah ('arif) ketika mendengarkan al-Quran yang agung dan, khususnya, ketika mendengarkan surah al-Fatihah. Dan, pengalaman dan kenikmatan mereka yang berdesak-desakan di lumbung setan untuk mendengarkan musik sama sekali tidak ada apa-apanya dibandingkan pengalaman dan kenikmatan mereka yang berkumpul di hadapan Yang Maha Pengasih.
content_ar:
“ Aba Dawid #4985,

Chapter Tivo: The Secret of Salah les in devotion to الله

are filled with everything except الله. T’hus, praying merely keeps them from indulging in this worldly life that they love so much. They are easily observed from their manner of performing prayer; they pray in a rush and lack tranquility and calmness. The only thing that compels them to pray in the first place is that they know it is a must (obligation). However, their salah is deficient; they utter with their tongues that which does not conform to what is in their hearts. All the while, the voice of their heart echos, let’s get this sa/db over with! Therefore the difference between a petson whose comfort is found in the salah and a person whose comfort is found outside the salah is obvious and self-evident. The former finds salah to be a grace upon his heart through which he experiences tranquility, calmness and pleasure, whereas the latter finds salah akin to heavy chains shackling his limbs and a gloomy cell imprisoning his heart.

"Truly, those whose comfort is not found in saléh feel as if praying was a prison for their soul and chains confining their limbs from committing sins. Despite that, the salah of this type of people might be a means whereby their sins are forgiven and they receive a share of الله’s Mercy depending on how much servitude to الله they manage to demonstrate while praying. But on the other hand, it is also possible they might be punished for the deficiency in their salah. As for those whose comfort is to be found in salah, for them praying is the garden in which they find comfort, pleasure, tranquility, and the grace of their hearts and souls. It brings them nearer to الله and elevates their rank, so that they only have a reward similar to those that preceded, but they also enjoy a special rank that none ever had, by being brought near to الله, in addition to the mete reward they receive for the performance of their salah.

INNER DIMENSIONS OF THE PRAYER

2.7 From the benefits of Sa/ah is nearness to الله

It is customary for kings to promise reward and nearness to those whom they are pleased with. The magicians said to pharaoh,

Heisei abs “Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?”

and he answered that they would be rewarded and be among those near to him, saying OWNS KGS 556 “Yes, and, [moreover], you will be among those made

The example of the first type is like a man who entered the abode of the king but was unable to gain entrance to him as there was a barrier standing between him and the king, hence he was unable to see him or gain access to this person. The battier is an embodiment of his desires and lusts as well as the smoke screen of his hopeful wishes in this life; his heart is sick and his self is wrapped up in what it desires wanting only its immediate share in this life. It is for these reasons that all the while they pray they are in an unhappy state wherein they are not only unable to obtain any comfort, but are also empty of feelings of fear and hope in الله, which makes prayer a suffering for them that only ceases when they finish praying, as only then are they able to return to that in which they find their comfort (i.e. worldly affairs and pleasures),

Chapter Two: The Secret of Salah lies in devotion to الله

The example of the second type is like a man who entered the house of the king upon which the barrier screening him from the king is removed; thus he is able to find delight in looking at the king and being at his service and in his obedience. In return, the king lavishes upon him every form of grace and brings him near to himself, For all these reasons, he is unable to bear leaving the house and wishes to remain standing before him to enjoy the sweetness of being close to him, the tranquility that he experiences from it, and being held in esteem by الله while he basks in His good Words (i.e. Qur’an). He also enjoys being in such a state of humbleness and humility before Him, for which he invokes Him more and more while he is being showered with His graces from every direction, not to mention the calmness that his soul experiences while his heart and limbs are fully attentive to his Lord. He is pleased and comfortable, worshipping الله as if he sees Him, for He manifests Himself to him through His Words (.e. Que’An). It should thus come as no surprise that the most distressing thing for him is to have to leave all that (i.e. conclude the salah). And الله is the Guide and the Helper.

‘The aforementioned are just some brief glimpses and hints about

saléh and a disclosure of some of its subtle qualities and hidden gems.

Distinction between the people of a/-Sama‘and the people of Salah

I call upon the people (a/Sama') who indulge themselves in listening (to music and songs) and I ask them by الله, the One and only truthful ah worthy of worship, to tell me if the experience and enjoyment they attain from listening to songs could ever be equal to, or even vaguely similar to, such feelings and state of heart! Moreover, I ask them, by الله, to tell me whether their indulgence in songs would allow them at all to experience these sublime emotions and state of heart when they pray? In fact, I would like to know if they have ever even detected from afar the fragrance of such sublime emotions and experiences!

I will take the liberty of answering these questions on their behalf, and I swear by الله that the experience and enjoyment attained from praying is incongruous with, and bears not an iota of resemblance to the experience and enjoyment they attain from listening to songs.(I fear that I might end up writing a long chapter, otherwise I would have mentioned some of what they experience when listening to songs, in order to explain the nature of this type of listening.)

A person whose heart has life in it and who enjoys even a mini-

Chapter Three: Distinction between the people of alSama‘ and the people

mal level of understanding shall realise the difference between the experience and enjoyment attained from reciting the Words of الله (.c. Qur’in) and the experience and enjoyment attained from singing lyrics. And he shall recognise the difference between the experience and enjoyment attained from standing before الله, the Lord of all the worlds (in sa/ah) and the experience and enjoyment attained from standing before singers (and musicians). He will also understand the difference between the experience and enjoyment attained from comprehending the meanings of the Words of الله and the experience and enjoyment they attain from singing and enrapturement, which is the spell of fornication and the Qur’an of Satan. By الله, whenever the love of both tastes (ic. Qur’an and songs) enters the heart of a petson, only one of them can remain in the heart as one of them will expel the other; just as the daughter of رسول الله ﷺ (4g) and the daughter of the enemy of الله cannot ever be married at the same time to one man." And الله, Exalted be He, Knows best.

“| He quotes a part of a الحديث in which النبي ﷺ (3) disliked ‘All ibn Abi ‘Talib to marry the daughter of Abii Jabl.

Bukhari #2943 and Muslim #2449. This part is used as a metaphor to mean that the words of الله and the words of Satan cannot gather in one place.

It is only when the hearts that have deviated far from the guidance of النبي ﷺ (ig) and have abandoned what he, his الصحابة and the righteous Salaf were upon, become upright that they will regain the discernment through which they can experience the right enjoyment and sensation. ‘Ihe early generations used to find enjoyment in matters pertaining to الله, Exalted be He, such as: praying, reciting the Qur’an, contemplating on the Words of الله and listening to them, attending the assemblies of scholars and the gatherings of knowledge, fighting in battles for the sake of الله, enjoining the good and forbidding the wrongdoing, loving and despising people for the sake of الله, etc. However, the preference of the later generations, except those whom الله saved, became distorted and they sought enjoyment in musical instruments, songs, dancing, noise and clamour, while desisting from doing deeds that الله loves. Indeed, the experience and enjoyment attained from melodies is nothing like the experience and enjoyment attained from the Qur’an. The experience and enjoyment attained from musical instruments is nothing like the experience and enjoyment attained from reciting a/-Nir? and

al-My’minin. The experience and enjoyment attained from tunes is nothing like the experience and enjoyment attained from the a/- Zumar, and the expetience and enjoyment obtained from flutes is nothing like the experience and enjoyment attained from the aah,

“The Hour has come neat, and the moon has split [in two].”

“ Surah al-Saffit (37)

INNER DIMENSIONS OF THE PRAYER

The expetience and enjoyment of those who stand on their feet serving Satan is in nothing at all like the experience and enjoyment of those who stand on their feet serving the most Merciful while reciting a/-An‘an and al-ArGpf'.The experience and enjoyment of those in rapture upon being overwhelmed by music is nothing like the experience and enjoyment of the knower (“rif) when listening to the Great Qur’an and, in particular, when listening to a/-Fatibab. And, the experience and enjoyment of those who jostle in Satan’s barn to listen to music is nothing like the experience and enjoyment of those who crowd before the Most Merciful.
